data.title: NeuroOne targets at least $10.5M revenue in 2026 while advancing brain and pain therapy platforms
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NeuroOne Medical Technologies Corporation (NMTC) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Dave Rosa, President, CEO & Director, stated the company is projecting fiscal year 2026 sales to be "at least $10.5 million, which is a minimum 17% increase from fiscal year 2025." He noted sequential sales growth and highlighted continued adoption of the OneRF brain ablation system, reporting that "the number of ablations performed in fiscal Q1 2026 was nearly half of all ablations performed since the launch of the technology."
  • Rosa shared that a registry is being established to capture patient outcomes with the OneRF system, with enrollment targeted by the end of the third fiscal quarter. He also pointed to international growth plans, saying the company is working towards ISO 13485 certification as "a first step towards commercializing internationally."
  • The company received FDA 510(k) clearance for the OneRF trigeminal nerve ablation system in August 2025. Rosa reported "the first 2 patients were treated at the University Hospitals Cleveland with both patients reporting pain relief," and to date, "9 total patients have been treated at 3 centers and all are reported pain free."
  • Rosa announced an accelerated drug delivery program, expecting devices to be available for commercial use and clinical studies in Q3 fiscal 2026, "approximately 6 months sooner than originally expected."
  • On partnerships, Rosa indicated ongoing "diligence discussions with a strategic to potentially license" the trigeminal nerve ablation technology and similar talks for the lower back pain programs.
  • Rosa highlighted the appointment of Jason Mills to the Board of Directors, noting his expertise in business development and recent role at Penumbra Inc.
  • Ronald McClurg, Chief Financial Officer, reported: "Product revenue was $2.9 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to product revenue of $3.3 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2025...On a sequential basis, revenue increased 5.5% from $2.7 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025."

Outlook

  • The company is projecting fiscal 2026 revenues of "at least $10.5 million," which Rosa described as "a minimum 17% increase from fiscal year 2025."
  • Rosa stated, "we expect to complete this limited launch [of trigeminal nerve ablation] by the end of Q2 fiscal 2026" and aims for the first enrollment in the OneRF registry by the end of Q3 fiscal 2026.
  • Drug delivery devices are expected to be "available for commercial use and investigational clinical studies or animals in Q3 fiscal 2026."

Financial Results

  • McClurg stated, "Product revenue was $2.9 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to...$3.3 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2025...On a sequential basis, revenue increased 5.5% from $2.7 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025."
  • He added, "Product gross profit was $1.6 million or 54.2% of revenue in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to...$1.9 million or 38.9% of revenue in the same quarter of the prior fiscal year."
  • Total operating expenses for Q1 fiscal 2026 were $3.3 million. Net loss was $1.4 million, or a loss of $0.03 per share.
  • Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2025, were $3.6 million. The company had working capital of $6.8 million and "no debt outstanding." McClurg said NeuroOne is "funded through fiscal 2026, potentially longer if key milestones are hit."

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann, asked about the number of sites for trigeminal nerve ablation and strategic interest. Rosa replied, "the 9 cases that we've done were done at 3 centers...all 9 cases, the patients were reportedly being free...in terms of strategic interest, yes, there are discussions underway."
  • Cohen asked about clarity on Zimmer's domestic presence. Rosa responded, "we don't have that information...we don't have clarity on the number of centers or the number of cases."
  • Cohen asked about OpEx expectations. McClurg answered, "SG&A would be fairly flat the rest of the year, but we do expect that the R&D may fluctuate..."
  • Cohen clarified the timing for animal studies. McClurg agreed, "it will pick up a little bit."
  • Jeremy Pearlman, Maxim Group, asked for clinical feedback on product use. Rosa said, "the device is very successful...in either reducing the amount of pain...or completely eliminating them."
  • Pearlman inquired about regional traction. Rosa replied, "that information, we don't have."
  • Pearlman questioned sales and marketing responsibility. Rosa confirmed, "the responsibility for all marketing and sales costs lie with Zimmer."
  • Pearlman requested a revenue breakdown. McClurg explained, "most of the revenue is what you're calling restocking...pretty much everything since then has been the continuation in restocking."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts posed detailed, sometimes pressing questions about product adoption, strategic partnerships, and OpEx, but generally maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on clarity and future growth prospects.
  • Management tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing progress and strategic milestones, but showed some caution and deference to partners when responding to questions about data and commercial details, using phrases such as "we don't have that information."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained neutral to slightly positive, while management's tone shifted from highly optimistic about record results to a more measured, operational focus and cautious optimism about future milestones.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal 2026 is now explicit, with a stated revenue target of at least $10.5 million, whereas the previous call deferred fiscal 2026 guidance pending Zimmer’s forecast.
  • Strategic focus shifted towards accelerating the drug delivery platform and advancing lower back pain programs with multiple diligence discussions underway.
  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more on real-world adoption, strategic partnerships, and clarity on revenue sources, whereas the previous quarter included more questions on development timelines and distribution agreements.
  • Operating expenses increased slightly from $3.2 million to $3.3 million, and cash reserves declined from $6.6 million to $3.6 million. Net results moved from a prior-year profit to a net loss this quarter, driven largely by the absence of $3 million in prior license revenue.
  • Management’s tone in the current quarter was still confident but more focused on execution rather than celebrating inflection points.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing diligence discussions with strategic partners for licensing technology but indicated that, should these not materialize, commercialization would proceed independently.
  • R&D expenses may fluctuate, especially with the accelerated drug delivery project and upcoming animal studies.
  • Cash reserves decreased but management stated funding should support operations through fiscal 2026, contingent on meeting key milestones.
  • There remains a lack of detailed data on adoption rates and regional traction, as reported by partners.

Final Takeaway

NeuroOne Medical Technologies Corporation outlined a strategy centered on advancing its core brain and pain ablation platforms, accelerating its drug delivery program, and expanding internationally. The company set a fiscal 2026 revenue target of at least $10.5 million, reflecting confidence in continued product adoption and upcoming commercial launches, with ongoing diligence discussions for strategic partnerships and a focus on disciplined cost management supporting operations through the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bit Digital sees peak short interest among crypto firms with up to $2B market cap in mid-Feb
data.description:

data.title: Beverage buzz: PepsiCo launches its prebiotic soda nationally
data.description:
PepsiCo (PEP) announced on Tuesday that its Prebiotic Cola is now available nationwide in stores and online. The food and beverage giant has described the product as the first prebiotic cola in the traditional cola set. The product is framed as delivering “unbelievably Pepsi” taste with no artificial sweeteners, 30 calories, 5 grams of sugar and 3 grams of prebiotic fiber per 12-ounce can. The national release features  original and cherry vanilla varieties in both single 12-oz cans and 8-packs.

Notably, PepsiCo (PEP) is using Prebiotic Cola to extend its core blue-can brand into the same gut-health and modern soda territory already occupied by newer players such as Poppi and Olipop. The aim is to bring younger, health-oriented consumers back into the traditional Pepsi system. The launch follows PepsiCo's (PEP) nearly $2 billion acquisition of Poppi in 2025 and slots alongside that brand, with Pepsi Prebiotic Cola offering slightly higher fiber per can than Poppi and being marketed as a major innovation in the cola category.

The prebiotic soda subcategory itself is relatively young, emerging in the late 2010s and early 2020s as brands like Poppi and Olipop repositioned soda as a “functional” beverage rather than a guilty-pleasure treat, using prebiotic fiber, natural flavors, and reduced sugar as the core value proposition. Market data for digestive health soft drinks in the U.S. show the segment more than doubling from roughly $197M in 2020 to more than $500M in 2025. Rival Coca-Cola (KO) is also in the category with Simply Pop, a prebiotic soft drink under the Simply Juice umbrella that is positioned against Olipop and Poppi and offers 6 grams of prebiotic fiber, vitamin C, zinc, and no added sugar.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ZIM, CMPS, DHR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were slightly higher on Tuesday amid a holiday-shortened trading week, spotlighting key economic data and corporate earnings ahead.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • ZIM Integrated Shipping (ZIM) +31% - Shares jumped after Hapag-Lloyd (HPGLY) agreed to acquire the shipper for $35 per share in cash, a 58% premium that values the company at about $4.2B. The deal still needs approval from regulators, shareholders, and the government of Israel, which holds a strategic “golden share” in ZIM. To help secure approval, the buyer included FIMI Opportunity Funds in the transaction.
  • COMPASS Pathways (CMPS) +38% - Shares surged after the company announced it successfully met the primary endpoint in its second Phase 3 trial of COMP360 psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression, strengthening the clinical case for the therapy and boosting expectations around a potential regulatory pathway and commercialization prospects.
  • Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) +9% - Shares advanced after a report that Elliott Investment Management has built a stake exceeding 10%, making it one of the company’s largest shareholders and positioning it to push for operational and strategic changes. According to the report, Elliott aims to improve financial performance and guest experience as the cruise operator faces pressure from softer premium travel demand, higher maintenance costs, fuel price volatility, and broader macro uncertainty.
  • Fiserv (FISV) +3% - Shares rose following a report that activist Jana Partners has built a stake and is pushing for steps to lift the stock, including accelerating growth in the core banking franchise and reviewing non-strategic assets. The investor is said to support the firm’s “One Fiserv” strategy and CEO Mike Lyons. Jana is not expected to seek a split of the payments and fintech businesses. The size of the stake and any board ambitions remain unclear.

Biggest stock losers

  • Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL) -24% - Shares plunged even after reporting positive topline results from its SOL-1 Phase 3 superiority trial of AXPAXLI for wet AMD, as investors appeared to focus on uncertainty around commercialization timing, durability of benefit, and next regulatory steps despite the study meeting its primary endpoint.
  • First Majestic Silver (AG) -9% - Mining stocks faced heavy selling pressure on Tuesday, caught in a "perfect storm" of thin Asian holiday liquidity and a commodity price correction eroding investor confidence. With Chinese markets closed, metals slipped - gold slipped toward $4,900/oz and silver fell toward $75/oz, while a stronger U.S. dollar, easing geopolitical tensions, and elevated global copper inventories further weighed on sentiment. Notable decliners included Hycroft Mining (HYMC), Aura Minerals (AUGO), Silvercorp Metals (SVM), Endeavour Silver (EXK), Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW), Harmony Gold (HMY), Hudbay Minerals (HBM), New Gold Inc. (NGD), Fortuna Mining (FSM), Hecla Mining (HL), Perpetua Resources (PPTA), Coeur Mining (CDE), and Eldorado Gold (EGO), all down 6%-10%.
  • Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) -4% - Shares slipped after the company agreed to acquire BHP’s (BHP) 33.75% silver stream at the Antamina mine for $4.3B in cash, lifting its total share to 67.5%. The deal, effective April 2026, is expected to add ~6 Moz of annual silver output in the first five years, raise 2026 production by ~11%, and expand reserves. The deal will be funded through cash, debt, and credit facilities, leaving net debt near $2.4B at closing.
  • Danaher (DHR) -3% - Shares slipped after agreeing to acquire Masimo (MASI) for $180 per share in cash, valuing the deal at about $9.9B. Masimo surged 34%, with the offer representing roughly a 38% premium to Friday’s close, signaling a strong shareholder uplift. The transaction, subject to customary approvals and closing conditions, is expected to be completed in the second half of 2026.

More on related stocks:

  • ZIM Integrated: Hapag-Lloyd Offer Makes Sense For Everyone, Will It Go Through?
  • Ocular Therapeutix: Considering Their Phase 3 'Superiority' Trial's Prospect
  • ZIM Integrated Shipping: Why The Buyout Matters Most - Initiating Strong Buy
  • Hapag-Lloyd to buy ZIM Integrated Shipping in $4.2B deal
  • FDA push to fast-track Compass Pathways’ psychedelic treatment rejected by Trump officials, STAT News reports

data.title: Figma teams up with Anthropic to convert AI code into designs
data.description:
In a move to stay ahead of the widespread investor concerns of artificial intelligence applications displacing creative design software, Figma (FIG) has partnered with Anthropic (ANTHRO), a leading AI model developer.

They have developed "Claude Code to Figma." It allows users to bring Claude Code workflows directly into Figma, which can convert the code into editable frames on the Figma canvas. 

"With Claude Code to Figma, each captured screen can be sent or copied to your clipboard and pasted into any Figma file," said Gui Seiz, Figma's design director of AI, in a blog post. "From there, it becomes a Figma frame you can organize, duplicate, refine, and share alongside the rest of your design work. Teams can do more than just view the UI: they can annotate what's working, call out what's unclear, and explore divergent ideas without needing to ask someone to context switch into a new environment or make changes that would require editing half of your code files."

Users can also capture multiple screens in a single session to allow for faster creation and a more in-depth exploration process for designs.

For Anthropic, this latest offering expands on its existing partnerships with a wide range of software providers. Last week, Anthropic completed a $30B Series G funding round, which gave the startup a $380B valuation. The company, which competes against the likes of OpenAI (OPENAI) and xAI (x.AI), has been working with advisors in advance of a potential initial public offering later this year.

data.title: BitGo Holdings gets new Buy rating at Citi
data.description:
Citigroup initiated coverage of BitGo Holdings (BTGO) with a Buy/High Risk rating, highlighting the crypto company's plan to shift toward a broader institutional infrastructure business from its core custody offerings.

The strategy is intended to pull in deeper wallet-to-workflow integrations and generate more revenue from a broader share of client spending. The CLARITY crypto infrastructure bill being hammered out in Congress should also benefit BitGo.

"With CLARITY, we believe BitGo’s role can flourish with the advent of regulated DeFi and tokenization of RWAs – likely affording strategic value to the name," analyst Peter Christiansen wrote in a note to clients.

"We agree with the 'picks and shovels' enabler characterization, though caveat with BitGo’s exchange-like crypto exposure," he added.

The Buy rating is more bullish than the average SA Analyst rating  of Hold.

Still, BitGo (BTGO) stock slid 10% in Tuesday morning trading as bitcoin (BTC-USD) dipped 2.7% to ~$67.0K, not far above its 52-week low of $60.2K.

data.title: Trump administration may change steel and aluminum tariffs, Greer says
data.description:
President Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminum have been "incredibly successful," but he is open to modifying its broad tariffs on steel and aluminum products, U.S. Trade Representative Greer said Tuesday on CNBC.

"You may want to sometimes adjust the way some of the tariffs are applied for compliance purposes," Greer said in a CNBC interview. "We've heard stories of companies that have had to hire extra people for compliance. We're not trying to have people do so much bean counting they're not running their company correctly."

The Trump administration reportedly has been working to narrow the scope of the tariffs, which not only include the metals themselves but also dozens of products that contain them.

Greer said the underlying metals tariffs will remain, calling them "very successful" in boosting the domestic industry. "Clearly, those are going in the right direction. They're going to stay in place," he said in the interview.

Last year, the U.S. imposed a 50% tariff on foreign steel and aluminum, a move that was intended to hit Chinese overcapacity but also wound up impacting other major trading partners hard, including Canada, Mexico, South Korea, and the European Union.

In early trading Monday: Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) -3.1%, Nucor (NUE) -2.2%, Steel Dynamics (STLD) -2.2%.

ETFs: (SLX), (XME)

data.title: Accenture gets rating upgrade at Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded Accenture's (ACN) rating to Overweight from Equal Weight while maintaining its $275 price target on the stock.

Shares of Accenture rose about 1% on Tuesday. 

Analysts led by Jason Kupferberg said they upgraded the rating on: fiscal second-half revenue acceleration; conservative fiscal 2026 guidance; overblown AI fears; and valuation.

The analysts added that recent booking trends are encouraging, and Accenture could be poised for solid fiscal second-quarter results next month.

The Irish consulting services provider is slated to report its fiscal second-quarter results on March 19.

The analysts noted that they have increased confidence in fiscal second-quarter constant currency revenue growth acceleration, given two consecutive quarters of improved bookings growth, positive incremental discretionary spending commentary from peers, and easier comps.

In addition, the analysts said they view Accenture's fiscal 2026 revenue guidance (which at the high end assumed no improvement in discretionary spending) as conservative. The analysts think the recent AI fears (spilling over from Software) are overblown and added that the valuation is attractive.

"Shares have been excessively punished recently due to latest round of AI fears, spilling over from anti-software sentiment. While ACN is not immune to AI headline risks we see ACN as well-positioned to emerge as a net beneficiary (sticky C-level client relationships, track record of navigating past tech paradigm shifts). CC revs [revenues] growth accel [acceleration] would support this thesis, catalyzing multiple expansion. F1Q AI-related revs/bkgs [revenue/bookings] grew 120%/83% though ACN is discontinuing such disclosures going forward," said Kupferberg and his team.

data.title: GSK says RSV shot Arexvy lowers risk of CV events, asthma, and COPD
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) said that real-world data of its respiratory syncytial virus vaccine Arexvy showed that it led to a reduction in major cardiovascular events, as well as reduced the risk of asthma and COPD flare-ups, compared to those who were unvaccinated. 
  • The retrospective cohort study of more than 2.5M US patients demonstrated 75.6% vaccine effectiveness against RSV-related hospitalization.
  • Other data indicated 63.1% vaccine effectiveness against major  cardiovascular events, such as heart attack and stroke, during RSV-related hospitalization among adults who were at least 60 years old. Also, vaccine effectiveness was, respectively, 74.4% and 61.6%  against severe COPD and asthma flare-ups during RSV-related hospitalization in the same age group.
